title: Gibeon: Where the Sun Stood Still: The Discovery of the Biblical City
author: James B. Pritchard
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted excavation synthesis. Project stores its own summary of the identification evidence only; no reproductions of photographs, drawings, or inscribed-handle transcriptions.
summary: Pritchard's synthesis of his University of Pennsylvania Museum excavations at el-Jib (Tell el-Jib), 1956-1962, reporting the site's Iron Age pool and rock-cut water-tunnel system and the jar handles recovered there inscribed with the Hebrew triliteral gb'n ('Gibeon'), first published individually in his 1959 monograph 'Hebrew Inscriptions and Stamps from Gibeon.' The inscribed handles are the epigraphic basis for identifying el-Jib with biblical Gibeon, including the pool referenced in 2 Samuel 2:13.
confidenceNotes: Bibliographic details (title, publisher, year, handle count) assembled from secondary/tertiary summaries (Wikipedia 'Al Jib jar handles' article; Penn Museum Expedition Magazine pieces), not from direct inspection of Pritchard's original monographs. TO VERIFY: exact handle count across seasons (secondary sources give figures ranging from roughly 30 to 60+) and page-level citation once a copy of the 1959 or 1962 volume is consulted directly.
